summary: The Small Conjecture ('every small hereditary class has bounded twin-width') was disproved by Bonnet, Geniet, Tessera, and Thomassé in Twin-width VII (arXiv:2204.12330, 2022), who constructed a family of induced subgraphs of an infinite Cayley graph that is small (as labeled graphs) yet has unbounded twin-width. Prior to the disproof, Twin-width IV (arXiv:2102.03117, 2021) established a positive resolution in the restricted setting of hereditary classes of ordered graphs, where bounded twin-width and smallness (growth at most 2^{O(n)}) are equivalent.

since_posted.title: Twin-width IV: ordered graphs and matrices
since_posted.authors: Édouard Bonnet, Ugo Giocanti, Patrice Ossona de Mendez, Pierre Simon, Stéphan Thomassé
since_posted.claim: Resolves the conjecture for hereditary classes of ordered graphs by proving that bounded twin-width and growth at most 2^{O(n)} (smallness) are equivalent in this setting.

since_posted.title: Twin-width VII: groups
since_posted.authors: Édouard Bonnet, Colin Geniet, Romain Tessera, Stéphan Thomassé
since_posted.claim: Disproves the conjecture by constructing a family of induced subgraphs of an infinite Cayley graph that is small as labeled graphs but has unbounded twin-width.

internal_refs_verified.note: Abstract explicitly states the paper 'answers our small conjecture [SODA ’21] in the case of ordered graphs'; matches the attributed partial resolution.

Wikipedia (Twin-width article) explicitly confirms this paper disproves the Small Conjecture via induced subgraphs of a Cayley graph; direct PDF fetch was not parseable but corroborated by two independent external sources.

search_queries: twin-width small conjecture hereditary class bounded disproved 2022 2023 2024
notes: The general conjecture is disproved. The converse direction (every bounded twin-width class is small) was proved in the source paper and remains true. A positive analog holds for ordered graphs (Twin-width IV). The counterexample arises from group-theoretic constructions (Cayley graphs of finitely generated groups with infinite twin-width).
